title: Brewers' Cody Ponce to Undergo Imaging for Knee Discomfort Amidst MLB Trade Rumors
body:
Toronto Blue Jays right-hander Cody Ponce's highly anticipated return to the major leagues ended in disappointment on Tuesday, as he was forced to exit his first start in five years due to knee discomfort. The 29-year-old pitcher, who has been working his way back to the top level after a prolonged absence, is set to undergo an MRI tonight to determine the extent of the injury. The news comes as a setback to the Blue Jays, who had been counting on Ponce to provide a boost to their depleted pitching rotation.

Ponce's injury occurred during the third inning of his outing, and he was unable to continue. The exact nature of the injury is still unknown, but the fact that he is scheduled for an MRI suggests that the team is taking a cautious approach. This is likely a prudent move, given the importance of Ponce's contribution to the Blue Jays' hopes for the remainder of the season. With a lack of depth in the rotation, losing Ponce for an extended period would be a significant blow.

The Blue Jays are also dealing with the loss of other key pitchers, including Alek Manoah, who is currently on the injured list with a shoulder issue. The team's pitching staff has been stretched thin, and Ponce's injury is a concerning development. However, the Blue Jays are not without options. The team has recalled right-hander Trent Thornton from Triple-A, and it is possible that other pitchers could be called up in the coming days.

In the context of Ponce's longer-term prospects, the injury is a setback, but it may not necessarily be a career-altering blow. Ponce has a history of overcoming adversity, including a bout of COVID-19 in 2020 that forced him to miss significant time. The right-hander has also shown flashes of brilliance on the mound, including a dominant performance in his major league debut in 2019. While the injury is a disappointment, it is unlikely to derail Ponce's career entirely.

The Blue Jays are likely to provide an update on Ponce's status in the coming days, after the results of the MRI are known. In the meantime, the team will continue to rely on its current pitching staff, which has shown signs of improvement in recent weeks. Whether Ponce is available to rejoin the rotation anytime soon remains to be seen, but one thing is certain: the Blue Jays will need to find a way to overcome this injury if they hope to remain competitive in the second half of the season.

As the Blue Jays look to the future, they will need to prioritize the health and well-being of their pitchers. With Ponce's injury serving as a reminder of the risks involved in the game, the team will likely take a more cautious approach to managing its pitching staff. Whether this approach will ultimately pay dividends remains to be seen, but one thing is clear: the Blue Jays will need to be resourceful and adaptable if they hope to succeed in the second half of the season.
